desc:
Just next to the trail rises the high green wall that forms the defence line of
the elven city. The golden trees grow high here, almost touching the sky as they
stretch their branches towards the heavens in friendly competition with the
neighbouring wall.
name: Forest near the Wall

desc:
This white platform rests comfortably among the branches high in a tree. Several
shelves stand along the edges, laden with exquisite armour and weapons - the
smith's personal favourites which are not for sale. The railing that encircles
the flet is made of a fine silver chain resting on smooth black iron posts from
which the light of the silver lamps is reflected in all directions.
name: Flet of the Armourers

desc:
Close to the wall the forest gives way and opens up into a small clearing. At
its centre stands a small forge with a roof supported by wooden pillars. Here
the smiths of the city craft their beautiful artefacts, as well as repair and
create the tools and weaponry that are used daily by the elves. The capacity of
the forge is not too large, but the workers are masters of their art and love
their craft.
name: A Small Forge

desc:
A large mallorn rises to the sky here. It stands in solitude, quiet and solemn,
as if left alone by the rest of the forest out of respect. Under its sprawling
branches the smiths and armourers of the Galadhrim meet to exchange knowledge
and teach their apprentices. Tools of all kinds lie neatly arranged on a small
wooden table standing at the foot of the tree. Placed in a semicircle around the
table are several benches of white marble. A small fire burns on a forge just
next to the table.
name: The Peleryn of the Armourers

desc:
On the northern slope of the high hill, close to the wall you hear the sounds of
a forge mixing with elvish songs, laughter and shouts reverberating through the
woods. The high trees standing here are crowded with flets, the wooden platforms
where the Galadhrim dwell. Ladders run up almost every tree. Several rope
bridges connect the tree-crowns.
name: Beneath the Flets of the Galadhrim

desc:
You find yourself by the wall on the northern slope of the hill, surrounded by
the tall mellyrn that are homes for the Galadhrim. Almost every tree rises
proudly towards the sky and carries several flets among its branches. From
within the greenery you can hear laughter and song, the soft language of the
elves whispering soothing words.
name: Beneath the Flets of the Galadhrim

desc:
Here, among the flets in the heart of the Galadhrim habitat, you are surrounded
by the sounds of everyday life. A child laughing, soft voices and soft singing
envelop you among the well-trodden paths. High up in almost every tree lie
several flets, making the treetops burst with life. Rope and wooden ladders
climb up the boles of several trees. High in the tree-crowns, rope bridges can
be seen running between the flets.
name: Beneath the Flets of the Galadhrim

desc:
You find yourself on the outskirts of Caras Galadhon, close to the towering wall
that surrounds the large hill. High up among the trees you can see some wooden
flets among the branches. On these platforms elves move about, singing softly or
laughing and shouting to each other. The trees open up into a large clearing
just to the west, but to the south the forest grows dense.
name: Beneath the Flets of the Galadhrim

desc:
High up in the air this large wooden platform rests securely in the branches of
a huge mallorn. The flet is furnished rather sparsely although still quite
functionally. Several sleeping mats lie rolled up in a corner and a table and
some benches stand in the centre. As a soft breeze gently blows through the
leaves, the outside light is dispersed and gives life to fantastic displays of
greenish light and shadows.
name: A Large Flet

desc:
You find yourself on the northern slope of the large hill between a winding
trail and the heart of the Galadhrim habitat. The large mellyrn rising high on
the slope below you are crowded with families living on the wooden platforms -
flets, that are so common among the forest-dwelling elves.
name: Beneath the Flets of the Galadhrim

desc:
Here, some way up the northern slope, you are in the middle of the everyday life
of the Galadhrim elves. Voices ring out from within the tall trees, calling out
from the lofty flets resting on the branches of the proud mellyrn. To the east
the forest opens up into a large clearing bustling with activity. A winding
trail passes through the forest just to the south.
name: Beneath the Flets of the Galadhrim

desc:
Although large, this clearing never feels empty. The trees around it fill the
air with soft singing of the birds, and the voices of the Galadhrim can be heard
faintly through the woods. Being in the centre of the Galadhrim habitat, the
clearing is a central gathering spot for the festivities in the city. In the
centre of the clearing stones are laid out, covering the ground so as not to
damage it when the great bonfires are lit during the feasts.
name: Clearing

desc:
An excellent view opens from the top of the great green wall that encircles the
fair city of Caras Galadhon. A well-trodden path atop the bulwarks, shielded by
protective crenellations, allows the elven archers to patrol the city and keep a
vigilant watch on the surrounding woods. Below, the golden forest stretches out
far in all directions, the only break in the green and gold being that of the
white stone road far below, running just outside the wall.
name: Northern Wall

desc:
Protecting Caras Galadhon, the broad city walls rise high, allowing a splendid
view and a perfect defensive position. Letting the eyes wander, one cannot help
feeling awed by the beauty of the land. The forest lying below, the white stone
road, the deep fosse encircling the city appear to be works of perfection, art
and harmony. A feeling of peace seems to fill the air as one gazes north into
the depths of the golden forest.
name: Northern Wall

desc:
Facing mainly north, the wall rises high above the ground. Below the wall lies
the great golden forest, spreading towards the horizon in all directions before
vanishing in a haze far far away. Closer by one can see the white stone road
that encircles the city and the deep fosse which forms the first line of defence
of Caras Galadhon.
name: Northern Wall

desc:
Broad and high, the impressive wall surrounding the city allows a few elves to
walk abreast easily. Curving softly, it faces northeast, giving a beautiful view
of the golden forest. The most prominent landmark lies at your feet - the deep
shadowed fosse, lying just outside the wall.
name: Northern Wall

desc:
This is where the tailors and seamstresses of Caras Galadhon sew, embroider and
mend the delicate clothes for the elves of the city, from the smallest child to
Lady Galadriel herself. Strips of silk and cotton and rolls of threads lie
scattered all around the platform. Scissors and needles are stuck into rolls of
threads or hung in pouches from the branches. In the back of the flet are many
buckets of dyes of all colours. The elves seem to favour most green, blue,
yellow, white and grey, similar to the colours of the golden forest, but other
dyes are available as well, for those that might have a fancy to use them.
name: The Tailors' Flet

desc:
Cradled in the midst of the strong branches close to the great grey trunk of the
tall mallorn is a small platform that is sparsely furnished. A low railing
encircles the flet and a few sleeping mats lie on the floor. A small chest for
personal belongings stands in a corner. A small table is in the centre of the
talan, surrounded by wooden chairs.
name: Platform in the Tree

desc:
High above the ground this platform lies cradled among the branches of the large
tree. The dense foliage forms a canopy that encloses the flet keeping the
inhabitants dry. A few sleeping mats lie neatly on the floor, and a large round
table with a few benches around it stands by the trunk of the tree. Two large
wooden chests and a fine bookshelf stand near the edge of the flet side by side.
name: A Small Treehouse

desc:
This large flet high up in the tree is scantily furnished with a few sleeping
mats, a small table and two benches. Besides this furniture, one can see a small
wooden chest standing near the edge of the platform and a bookshelf that stands
close to the trunk of the tree. The branches surrounding the flet offer some
shelter but still let the light filter in, brightly coloured by the leaves that
act as a roof. Rope bridges lead from the flet in all directions but north, and
a white ladder leads down to the ground.
name: A Flet

desc:
This large wooden platform high among the branches of a tall mallorn tree is
obviously the home of a few families. The flet is divided into a few smaller
parts, all of which are furnished in rather similar ways: a few sleeping mats, a
table or two, a few chairs or benches and a couple of wooden chests. A ladder
leads up to another flet along the bole of the tree.
name: On a Large Flet

desc:
High above the sloping ground, where the elves of Caras Galadhon reside, wooden
platforms, known to the elves as flets, rest amid the thick branches of the tall
trees. This platform is neatly furnished. A few sturdy wooden beds and several
sleeping mats indicate that this is a large household. A finely carved wooden
chest standing off to the side gives an air of importance to the room, as does a
large table surrounded by carefully crafted chairs.
name: A Beautiful Flet

desc:
This white talan is rather small and contains only a a wooden desk, a chair and
a couple of bookshelves. Several rolled-up parchments lie on the desk. A quill
pen and a small flask of ink complete the image of a home of an elderly scholar.
name: High in a Tree

desc:
This large platform rests securely in the firm grip of the great tree. The flet
is big enough to house two families that have put up small screens to divide the
platform into smaller parts. Sleeping mats lie all over the floor and several
small chests stand along the low wooden railing.
name: On a Flet

desc:
The wooden walls of this dilapidated building have an indeterminate colour of a
rotting barrel. The crumbling ceiling beams support the earthen roof, with roots
of weeds hanging from it in foul congregations. The mud floor is layered with
trampled dirty hay. Rudimentary shelves line the walls, empty but for a few clay
mugs. A small window facing west lets in a modicum of light; opposite it is the
hut's only man-made entrance. The southern wall is gone, collapsed by the heat
of a raging forest fire.
name: A Solemn Hut

desc:
A narrow path littered with darkened stones leads away from a small mud hut and
up a gentle incline. The gargantuan trees in the surrounding forest hang over
the path ominously. Their black crowns drown out the sky. They hover above the
hut, their branches like giant tentacles ready to pounce at any moment. The
stones marking the path are dark with soot, though whence it came one cannot
tell.
name: Winding Path

desc:
Drowning among the decaying tall black trees of indeterminate origin is a small
path, twisting and turning among their humongous roots. The branches of the
trees, tantalizingly ugly, hang over the path precariously. They almost touch
the ash-covered ground in a few places. The twisted shadows of the surrounding
forest come alive in its darkness.
name: Path through a Dark Forest

desc:
Large black boulders, massive like the stones of Angband, are scattered about
the ground here. They impede the offensive of the dark wood that is pressing on
the rock-strewn path that twists from west to north. The high ground gives way
in the east with a sharp drop. The sound of water can be heard from below it.
name: Winding Path in the Dark Forest

desc:
Black water, deep and menacing, runs slowly southwards. Outlined by tall
unclimbable banks, the stream turns into tarred barely moving sludge along the
edges. Rotting pieces of wood and partial bones, of which few are of animal
origin, dot the shallow parts of the river. To the south it merges into a black
lake, and a wooden bridge is visible further north.
name: A Black Stream

desc:
Half-rotten rickety planks hammered haphazardly together are standing on the
tall black beams, rising high over the foetid water. The scaffolding that
supports the bridge has seen better times; one of its sections bends westwards
precariously. A noxious fog is rising above the black water, which from the
bridge looks bottomless and poisonous. A greenish frothy residue sticking around
the support beams grows half-way up to their tops.
name: Black Stream Crossing

desc:
This pool of pitch-black water, perfectly round like the eye of Ungoliant,
glares menacingly at the surrounding forest. Not one tree bole or branch is
suspended over the lake. Silence hangs over it like a shield, and there is an
almost physical feeling of danger permeating the air. How deep this ghastly pool
is one cannot guess, for the evil waters conceal everything and reveal nothing.
name: A Black Lake

desc:
This forest is a nightmare of twisted trees, roots, and shadows. Indeed its
shadows move with a life of their own and suck the colour from wherever they
fall. Delicate tapestries of webs, shining black with slime, congregate among
the lower branches and trunks of the crooked black trees. A low noise, humming,
hammering, hollering, and haranguing, emanates from all around here, giving the
place a certain feeling of genuine helpless insanity.
name: A Twisted Forest

desc:
Deformed trees of mighty girth crowd the forest till it becomes impenetrable in
most directions. A multitude of twisted branches spirals to the ground, almost
touching it. No underbrush grows here, but the charred roots of the trees plough
the ground tirelessly, though nothing but terror and nightmares will ever be
sowed here.
name: A Twisted Forest

desc:
After making a few final turns, the pebbled trail arrives at the feet of a set
of gigantic iron gates. The desolate landscape is open to the eye here. To the
west a stream flows at the bottom of a precipitous drop. Black stones of
volcanic origin are scattered along the path impeding the passage rather than
marking the trail. Giant footprints, each a dozen handspans long, are crowding
in front of the gate.
name: Winding Path

desc:
This trail, twisting and turning among disfigured black trees, is covered with
charred pebbles. Thin columns of smoke rise from among the larger stones. They
reek of peat bog, rotten wood, and burning flesh. To the west, the path crosses
a dark stream. To the east, walls of a mighty citadel rise starkly against the
sky. Hardy black flowers on short greying stalks glare evilly from the ground.
name: Winding Path

desc:
Incredibly tall and twisted dark trees, like the heart of Morgoth, grow here.
They stand file by file, rank by rank: a mighty army of darkness, a harbinger of
ruin, a scrounger of light. The reality warps itself among their trunks and
their shadows, and distance loses its meaning to a mortal eye. No movement, no
small forest animals, no life can subsist here.
name: Warped Woods

desc:
Tall trees, black, like the darkest moonless night, are standing here upright,
their branches shooting straight into the skies. Their breed, common to this
forest, seems to have been released of their twisted souls in death. The pungent
air hangs heavily over the puddles of water collecting at their feet.
name: Dead Trees

desc:
Dark and menacing, the trees in this part of the forest are twisted into strange
primeval forms. They are the spirits of the forest, the guardians of this
forsaken land, the invaders of dreams, the destroyers of hope. In their shadows,
no thing retains its true form. Under their boughs, all living things become
small and insignificant. Dread hangs over the forest here like a thick winter
cloak.
name: Twisted Forest

desc:
The crooked black trees, tall, mighty, and as ugly as the fires of Udun, have
planted their roots here. The roots themselves, their bulges and tentacles,
crooked fingers and twisted arms, cover the forest floor so thoroughly that no
hint of earth is seen anywhere. The darkness of the forest is permeated by a
rancid smell. It is windless here, but among the trees, their shadows move,
while their twins stand still, as if suspended in some dream of calamity and
horror.
name: A Dark Forest

desc:
The narrow winding path is twisting and turning among the ash-covered boulders
and contorted trees, whose limbs keep trying to reach the parched earth upon
which the trail is running. Darkness and the feeling of foreboding hang heavily
in the air. Remnants of isolated fires dot the ground, as if some great army
broke camp here on the way out. Some persistent weeds, twisted by their very
nature, much like their larger cousins, grow on both sides of the path.
name: Winding Path

desc:
The muddy ground here is covered with holes and burrows - ghastly sores on the
skin of the land. The monstrous trees growing in this forest are twisted into
demonic shapes. The stooped trunks of some of them bend so much that they reach
the ground with their deformed tops. An acrid smell rises from the hollows in
the ground; it is almost tangible in the heavy-hanging atmosphere of this
forsaken forest.
name: In the Twisted Forest

desc:
Roaring into the eternal twilights of the Angmarian sky, the massive iron gates
span the opening between the black walls of the outer citadel. The sheets of
iron are hammered together by square blackened nails whose caps bear rudimentary
engravings of an iris, malevolent even in its basic incarnation. Thirty feet
tall, the gates can move outwards on large metal wheels deviously concealed
within their structure. Sharp spikes run along their top edge, and the front
part of the gates is covered with them also, much like the skin of a porcupine.
This is the entrance to one of the mightiest dark strongholds, its main tower
silhouetted ghost-like against the eastern sky.
name: The Iron Gates

desc:
Black cobblestones, worn with age and flattened by feet and paws of many
Angmarian armies, line the square in front of the mighty iron gates. Bits of ash
swirl around the square like first winter snowflakes. A chill wind blows back
and forth, a captive within the tall black walls. A black menacing guard tower
rises to the east, and behind it the main citadel blots out the sky. A dark road
runs from here toward the keep's centre and along the perimeter walls.
name: Black Square

desc:
Darkened cobblestones, flat from all the feet that have marched along here over
the ages, run along the western outer wall of the castle. A couple of
dilapidated hovels hug the walls. The road turns from the north to the east
here, dodging the burned remains of army barracks. Inescapable, the smell of
droppings and human remains lingers in the air.
name: Dark Road

desc:
Propped against a black stone wall, this wooden contraption leans heavily on the
structure of its front wall. Its ceiling is surprisingly tall, its details
receding into animated darkness. Horizontal bars supporting the roof bear
leftovers of many rotting ropes. Crunching underneath, the dirty mud floor is
covered with thick piles of human remains. Soft screams, echoes of things past,
pierce the air with their parting pleas, forever captured by the dreadful magic
of this place. Ghastly and intimidating, this house is a graveyard of the ages.
name: Ghastly House

desc:
The inner road leaves the square in front of the gates and departs north. Wide
and spacious, it still seems insignificantly small compared to the walls
encircling the castle. The wall of the guard keep runs along its eastern side, a
stomach-turning smell of burning flesh coming strong from that direction.
Battlements are outlined high up along the castle walls.
name: Dark Road

desc:
The road turns along the northwestern corner of the walls of the Angmarian
castle. Several levels of heavily fortified battlements are visible all the way
to the top of the wall. Some tall metal ladders are heaped in the corner. They
would allow for quick access to the lowest floor of defence installations in
case of emergency. Link-chain ladders connect the rest of the levels among
themselves.
name: Dark Road

desc:
Countless couriers, prisoners, and armies trotted down this road before its dark
stones turned flat as they are now. The huge monoliths comprising the walls have
a bearing of terrifying prowess. They and the cobblestones seem to have come
from the same mine, dark, lifeless, but with a soul-weakening magic of its own.
The north walls of the citadel and the guard keep protect the road from the
elements both within and outside the Angmarian castle.
name: Dark Road

desc:
Bleak and dismal, the cheerless stone road is squeezed between two ominous black
walls, and thus is forever destined to be in their murky shadows. Nothing grows
or lives here, for the joyless stones of Angmar can provide no nourishment. In
fact, they are repositories of the darkest and foulest magic which holds this
land of nightmares together. The only break in the monotony is a small arched
passage to the south, leading in towards the keep.
name: Dark Road

desc:
The road passes from east to west here, running between the walls of the keep,
narrowing as it goes into the corner to the east. Rotten wooden scaffolding of
defensive nature rises several levels, leaning precariously along the northern
wall. The blackened cobblestones with puddles of dark foetid water look
particularly malignant.
name: Dark Road

desc:
An overhanging corner of the guard keep depresses this corner of the keep, where
the road narrows as it goes into a bend, and re-emerges, widening on its way to
the gate of the citadel proper. Piles of bones in the bend conceal the foetid
puddles that make this place reek far and high.
name: Dark Road

desc:
Vaulting impossibly high, while bending inwards ever so slightly, the inner wall
of the castle of Angmar rises to the east. The uneven surface of the citadel
defensive circle belies its true nature: the carved cliff surface on the western
side of a mighty mountain, a sister-rock of Gundabad. The road runs south here,
toward a set of gates that lead into the fortress.
name: Dark Road

desc:
Looming high above the courtyard, the inky-black wall of the keep looks down on
a small crossing. The murky road opens south into a dimly lit square while
allowing escape from the keep through an arched passage to the north. East, a
narrow but heavy door is set in the monolithic wall like an afterthought. Across
from it a darkened archway looms. Bloody stains cover the walls all around from
its foot to about the height of an ordinary human. Many a prisoner has been used
here for target practice by the keep's garrison.
name: Dark Road

desc:
Five tall birch stakes smoulder in the middle of the plaza. Lying in heaps
around them are copious amounts of burning firewood and bones. Skeletons chained
to the poles are not entirely fleshless yet, and the stench rising from them is
overwhelming. The silhouette of the guard keep is sketched in the smoky
twilights along the edges of the square. A blood-red picture of a crown appears
above the archway leading south, and north seems to be the sole way out of the
keep.
name: The Burning Stakes

desc:
The wooden walls of this house have rotten through in places revealing that the
whole structure is propped precariously between several rather impressive stone
walls. The western wall is missing entirely, the view opening out into a square
of burning stakes. The flat ceiling still holds its own. Several heavy-duty
metal hooks are screwed into it at regular intervals. The dirt floor has been so
heavily trampled down over the years, that nothing leaves tracks anymore on it.
One has to be careful to not tumble into the large round hole dominating the
centre of the floor.
name: Guard House

desc:
This hole of a place is not very deep but dank and terrifying by implications
written in the bones scattered on its floor, the graffiti painted in blood on
its walls, and the scratch marks all along the perimeter belonging to something
heavy and unknown. Above, a narrowing escape leads outside. Midway up, two
crossed bars are wedged securely in it.
name: Dark Pit

desc:
The painted narrow door in the western wall belies the appearance of this
chamber. Dark and oppressive, this square room has a single torch burning with
fierce white fire on the wall opposite of the entrance. Pointed ribs rise from
the corners of the room to form a single cross vault, receding into the darkness
above. A few crude metal implements are attached to the stone walls all around
the room.
name: Dark Chamber

desc:
Two rows of thick hexagonal columns run from the room's only entrance in the
eastern wall and into the darkness away from it. The high ceiling is composed of
two barrel vaults on the sides and a sequence of cross vaults, running from the
top edges of the columns, all along the middle of the hall. Heavy chandeliers
hang from the points where the ribs of the ceiling vaults meet. From there, a
multitude of candles casts its ghostly light all across the guard hall. Wooden
benches line the walls with tables, hastily cobbled together, standing in front
of them filling the nave.
name: Black Hall

desc:
The round base of the keep's tower is bare but for a staircase and two archways.
To the north the pointed archway, decorated with sooted runes along the outer
edge, leads to the square outside. To the right of the entrance, an entryway to
a tall tunnel with a rounded roof recedes further into the keep. The hall
encompasses two flights of stairs that lead further up: the lower one is facing
the entrance, the one above it faces away from it. The staircase is constructed
of solid stone blocks. The chunk of it facing away from the square has holes in
its steps.
name: Inside a Towering Keep

desc:
A grey stone column with hexagonal base widening toward the ceiling is standing
in the middle of this large room. Not a window is in sight. A stone bench runs
around the column; a few more benches line the walls. The corners of the room
wallow in darkness, which not even the bright ghostly torches hanging all around
it, can penetrate. A narrow tunnel with rounded roof departs into the western
wall.
name: Inside a Towering Keep

desc:
A wooden ladder propped against the wall is the only way out. It goes through
the square hole in the ceiling, where a shadow can be seen. This round room
hints at the shape of the building above it, but where in a regular room there
would be windows, here there are alcoves with decayed bits of paper and
sheepskin hinting at what once must have been a records room. Multiple torch
holders line the walls, though no torch has touched them in a long time.
name: A Dark Basement

desc:
This spacious round room has no windows, but two metal doors, mysteriously
bolted open to the walls and opening out to a balcony with guardrails of cast
iron, ornately silhouetted against the landscape. Another smaller doorway is
inserted into the eastern wall, and two staircases lead above and below this
hall. One is a sturdy stone staircase descending to the ground level. The other
is a narrow spiral staircase also of cast iron, in the same motif as the
balcony, leading to the tower above. Around the top of the walls, tattered
banners and standards hang, and rusted ceremonial weapons from lands long gone
line the walls below them.
name: Ceremonial Hall

desc:
The road leads away from the gates and towards the centre of the guard keep, but
curves away to the south circling the citadel. To the south, the road continues
along the southern edge of the outer castle walls. Gusts of chilly wind bring
smoke and smell here from the direction of the keep. The walls of the area are
covered with soot; what colour its stones once were, one cannot tell.
name: Dark Road

desc:
Black cobblestones cover the ground here. Droppings and excrement of indistinct
origin lie in deep puddles. The stench is overwhelming, compounding the feeling
of despair that soaks this place through every stone. Darkness reigns the skies
where they are not concealed by the castle walls, the keep, or the main citadel.
The road runs south here, along the perimeter walls.
name: Dark Road

desc:
Turning between the tall black walls on both sides, the road narrows a little
here. The black stones covering it are worn with age, same as the walls of the
castle. Ashes are piled in small mounds where they meet, as if trying to avoid
the chilly touch of the wind whistling shrilly all over the castle keep.
name: Dark Road

desc:
Skirting the southmost edge of the keep, this road has been used for ages for
massing the armies. Envoys and friends of the Witch-king, his prisoners and
slaves, came this way, some of them never to return to the outside world. Only
the cold wind, loud, shrill, and wordless moves here - the only witness of their
chilly fate.
name: Dark Road

desc:
The dirty cobblestoned road runs between two tall walls - the wall of the guard
keep to the north and the outer wall of the castle to the south. Several broken
wheelbarrows without wheels are perched along the sides together with some
crushed ladders. Decrepit remnants of some wooden defensive construction rest
along the southern wall.
name: Dark Road

desc:
Two mighty walls come together at a straight angle here. They rise into the
darkened sky like two stormy waves, suspended forever in their act of
insurrection. The road continues west and north of here, just as dirty and
putrid as it is here. The inner wall of the guard keep to the northwest leans
subtly outwards, and thus overhangs the road ever so slightly.
name: Dark Road

desc:
The craggy mountain surface to the east would have concealed any hint of
sunrise, even if it was not so impossibly dark here. The malevolent presence
that haunts the castle seems to grow in that direction. The cold clammy walls
accumulate moisture around it, and a thin layer of fog clings to it also. The
road runs north here, leading to the gates that protect the main part of the
citadel.
name: Dark Road

desc:
Across from the back wall of the guard keep, the first level of castle defence,
rise the gates of the citadel of Angmar. Opening outwards, they are made to look
much like the cliff surface surrounding it. Upon closer inspection, it turns out
that they are made of metal. The metal sheets are piled on one another, until
each gate half is more than a foot thick. They ring unendingly like chimes, as
if singing for the forgotten souls that have entered this Vala-forsaken place
never to emerge again. The road runs south and north here, along the walls and
around the keep. Upon the gates, a giant ring hangs with a human skull hanging
off the chain within it - a giant bell that is no more used for knocking than it
is for decoration.
name: Gates to the Citadel

desc:
Directly inside the gates five niches are carved into the eastern wall of this
checkpoint, where the guards can take stock off newcomers. The old rusted spikes
peek from the floor, though it is clear that the mechanism that activates them
into the feet of unwelcome guests is broken. The gates are covered with a layer
of wood that muffles the sounds coming from outside, and now the oaky rotten
smell from it permeates this place.
name: Checkpoint

desc:
Dark stains run down the walls that once had intricate geometric designs painted
into them in white and red. The passage is quite wide here; enough to let six
people walk shoulder to shoulder. The dilapidated rounded ceiling is missing a
few stones. While insufficient to make it cave in, this allows the rainwater and
humidity from eternal mountain fogs to enter the castle. Here it is absorbed by
old spongy walls and in turn breeds some especially resilient forms of mould.
name: Passage

desc:
Hanging on heavy chains from the ceiling, columns of three beds each are
suspended along the northern, eastern, and western walls. In the middle of the
room, a set of weapon and arrow racks serves as a miniature armoury for guards
attached to this post. The racks are bolted fast to the floor, and are the only
feature well-visible through the open doorway.
name: Guard Chamber

desc:
The clean straight lines of two perpendicular passages intersect here. A
diagonal line slashes across the floor where they meet, and their ceilings form
a saddle from which a heavy chandelier hangs. A dozen spearheads peek straight
out of the corner, all along the length of the wall crease. One would be unwise
to lean that way. A faded solemn geometric motif that was once red and white
runs at shoulder height along the walls on both sides.
name: Passage

desc:
The castle passage leads eastwards here, heading deeper into the stronghold of
Angmar. The smooth walls meet above in a rounded ceiling - a complicated
arrangement of bricks that are holding up without any underlying support even as
the pathway twists here. A small window, narrow and pointed both at the top and
bottom, showcases Misty Mountains in all their cloudy magnificence, dark shaded,
and shimmering ever so slightly. The view down reveals a precipitous drop and a
foggy mountain valley with no bottom, for in it a cloud sleeps comfortably for
all times.
name: Passage

desc:
The dark corner of the passage harbours a few spiders, a lot of dust, and a
legion of ghosts from the past. These walls are silent witnesses to the terrors
and atrocities of the Witch-king, as a tapestry hanging on the northern wall
depicts in great detail. Woven into its soiled surface in red, white, and black
is the story of the Witch-king's victory over the people of Arthedain in
Fornost. Occasional gusts of wind disturb the back of the tapestry, for it is
nailed over a north-facing window.
name: Passage

desc:
No dust is allowed to collect in the passageways of the castle, for the patrols
sweep it endlessly, and messengers go back and forth between the high sanctuary
of the King and the castle's environs. The wide, dark floor stones are polished
smooth by their feet. The pockmarked walls, their close cousins, confine this
place from east and west, connected above by a well-proportioned barrel roof. A
guard check point is situated south of here.
name: Passage

desc:
A defensible guard checkpoint bottlenecks the passage here. It is wider than the
rest of the hallway, and has a strangely checkerboarded floor. Spears hang along
its eastern wall, rusted to their holdings. A small guard chamber west of here
offers a convenient place to watch the traffic without being in the way.
name: Checkpoint

desc:
The sterile passageways of the castle attract gloom like a great ageless sponge.
Light fails to dissipate the shadows in its corners. Shedding paint on the walls
reveals porous limestone behind it, but the floor is made of some other stone.
Dark and well-preserved, it is able to withhold the onslaught of both feet and
ages, and remain ice cold forever.
name: Passage

desc:
Going onwards, seemingly forever, this dark passage looms over the heads of the
travellers. The walls are made of strange black stone, ice-cold to the touch.
Dust gathers in the corners and small spider webs can be spotted in the ceiling.
The floor is smooth, as thousands of feet have trodden this pathway over the
centuries. The dry air is permeated with decay and strange unpleasant smells
that bring to mind unvoiced terrors of childhood dreams.
name: Passage

desc:
The darkened brick walls of this room are lined with chains hanging from the top
of the ceiling. A rusty metal grid is hanging on one hinge to the right of the
entrance. A pile of bones, looking rather mislaid in a military citadel, is
collecting dust in the corner. Clinking and clanging of the chains, coming from
some hidden breeze, breaks up the terrible monotony of this place.
name: Guard Chamber

desc:
The dark passageway makes a sharp turn here, from west to north. The walls are
composed of a dark stone, ever ice-cold to the touch. In the southeast corner
stands a large pillar made of a single solid piece of marble. From the floor all
the way to the roof, carved out of lime-coloured stone, human figures in
agonising pain reach out their hands as if asking for redemption.
name: Passage

desc:
Leading ever deeper into the fortress, the passage continues in a south to north
fashion. At one place, it gets narrower, hampering fast movement. The dark stone
walls soak up any noise, leaving one lingering in utter silence. Terrifying, the
silent arrow slits face the inside of the passage: what the invisible archers of
Angmar look like is left to one's imagination.
name: Passage

desc:
The dark hallway here has been constructed in a way that impedes travel, so it
is easier to defend the passage. The hallway continues north and south while a
large chamber opens up eastwards. The walls loom overhead, a heavy burden on any
traveller's soul. There is no movement in the cold air which stinks of death and
decay.
name: Checkpoint

desc:
Large and open, this chamber dwarves any creature in its bowels. Except, that
is, for the ruler on the raised dais along the eastern wall, who can look down
upon its vassals from the steel throne high above the ground. The dais is
covered with carvings, all depicting crushed and broken men, maimed and in
agonising pain.
name: Audience Chamber

desc:
In the glare of massive ghostly torch flames, the narrow corridor carries a
sense of pending doom, originating, perhaps, from an overbearing sense of
claustrophobia. The passage is narrow and tall. The eerie lighting spreads above
the brass braziers, but never reaches the ceiling, while the floor is just as
impenetrable in this unholy darkness.
name: Passage

desc:
This spacious room would normally be home to a small squad of guards. These
days, it stands deserted, beds taken off chains and stacked in the corner. The
darkened sooty ceiling is low, much lower than in the rest of the castle. The
door that formerly blocked this room from the chamber beyond stands in the
opposite corner, its cast-iron grid rusted through in several places. An old
white skeleton is chained to it at the top.
name: Guard Chamber

desc:
Time and rust took toll on all the racks and weapons strewn around this chamber:
there is not a usable piece of metal to be seen. This room abuts a magnificent
audience chamber with a sinister throne visible if one glances to the right of
the crumpled doorway. A few chandeliers are piled in the corner, rusted to each
other and to the iron-laid floor.
name: Guard Chamber

desc:
Unusually delicate carvings adorn the cavernous walls of the corridor here. But
while they are exquisite in nature - they are not at all fine in content:
dreadful ghasts, malignant spirits, and a variety of abominable phantasms, their
eyes glittering with a multitude of semi-precious stones, glare at the passersby
from their lofty perches under the ceiling. Sleeping chambers to the east of
here is what they are guarding, whether to keep living things in or out.
name: Passage

desc:
This circular room has a radius of about twenty yards. The walls are draped in
red linen giving it a soft feel that clashes with the rest of the complex. The
roof is shaped like a dome, and at the top of the span a small hole is outlined,
where the polluted air of this chamber can escape. What remains of the door that
once occupied the western doorway, lies scattered on the floor. Four beds stand
along the walls, though time has taken its toll on them. Close to the doorway
stand a rack and a cabinet where the guests could leave their belongings. A
wooden table with six sturdy chairs occupy the centre of the room.
name: Guest Chambers

desc:
The tall arched windows of this elongated rectangular hall open into a smoky
walled courtyard. A raised platform along the eastern wall houses charred
remains of a large bed with four decapitated bedposts. The flat ceiling of thick
wooden beams is lined with an assortment of metal hooks of all shapes and sizes.
name: Master's Hall

desc:
Piles of dead fish are rotting in the creekbed here. The smelly dirty water,
covered with some green foamy residue, is waist deep. No water flow can be
discerned. Here and there, dead tree branches stick out of the water, their
twisted fingers reaching out in the direction of every perceived movement. A
foetid smell suffuses the air, augmenting the underlying feeling of the land
rotting and wallowing in its own decay.
name: A Stagnant Creek

desc:
A round chamber, narrowing toward the middle of the ceiling into a cupola, has
several windows all around it. All of them are barricaded with sheets of metal,
firmly secured against any hint of light from outside, though a cold draft is
very much in evidence in this tower. Some hooks and chains hang from the ceiling
all the way to the floor, exposing this room manifestly for what it is - a
private interrogation room.
name: Tower Chamber

desc:
A semi-circular stone platform protruding from the wall is lined with a cast
iron guardrail, intricately shaped into spirals terminating in small
triple-spiked crowns. The balcony opens onto the smoke-filled courtyard, with a
good view of the iron gate to the west and any possible executions below. An
arched doorway leads back into the tower, but there are no visible doors keeping
unwanted visitors out.
name: Ornate Balcony

desc:
The rounded barrel-roof of the passage is stained black above the torch holders
lining the walls every foot. Ghostly blue flames flicker in them, but illuminate
nothing. The passage wiggles a few feet while keeping the general north to south
direction, but at the cusp of the bend, an unadorned doorway leads into an
abandoned guardroom.
name: Passage

desc:
This cramped square room with weapon racks bolted to the walls is a regular
guard chamber of the Angmarian castle. Several metal boards hanging on chains
from the ceiling, much like a ladder, constitute guard beds. Four guards can
take a nap on this rickety contraption while their comrades mull around the
guard room or roam the hallways. A few rusted hinges line the entryway, but the
door itself lies prone on the floor.
name: Guard Chamber

desc:
The passage bends here, and where the barrel roofs of its two arms meet, a
cross-saddle is formed. From its cusp hangs a cast iron chandelier with nine
candle holders at its rounded edge. Two crossed flat crowns span the middle of
the circle. South of here, the passage comes to a set of stairs leading down. A
small triangular platform of stone protrudes from the wall, spanning but a small
part of the corner like a small bench.
name: Passage

desc:
A wide circular staircase plunges to the level below. It is made entirely out of
cast iron, and begins with a foot-wide platform at the top, bolted to the floor
where the passage ends. Elaborate handrails run alongside the stair on both
sides. While cast iron is quite heavy, part of the burden is taken away from the
staircase since the steps themselves are not solid, but made of a cross-hatched
pattern of iron bars.
name: Descending Stairs

desc:
Deep under the Misty Mountains, unthinkable horrors lie, and while this place is
part of the castle made by men, its dark corners belie its origin. No care was
taken to smooth the wall of this dungeon, carved out in the rock of the
mountains. Sharp edges and pointed angles are everywhere, except the dusty floor
toward which a black iron staircase descends from above. A miniscule doorway
leads from here into a prison cell. A heavy wooden door with a couple of
eyeholes separates the two rooms.
name: Dank Dungeon

desc:
Trees, cracked and burned to ashes, stand broken, as skeletal fingers, in the
still raging fires. Trees not yet consumed glow with red-orange light and waves
of heat roll over the ground. The fire is spreading swiftly, with nothing in its
path being able to stop its devastating progress.
name: A Raging Forest Fire

desc:
Damp and dark, the dungeon floor is littered with the decaying remains of its
former inhabitants. The cold stone walls have faint markings in endless lines
and rows, at places covered in wet moss. Piles of bones lie thrown in one of the
corners. The only way out is through a sturdy wooden door, reinforced with thick
iron bars and carefully blocked from the outside.
name: A Prison Cell

desc:
The hallway comes to an abrupt end, while a steep stairway continues upwards.
The walls and roof lean inward, creating a narrow tunnel where a man would have
to bend slightly to be able to proceed. The steps are worn out, making the
ascent hazardous. A slight draft moves the dry, reeking, warm air upwards.
name: Ascending Stairs

desc:
Here, at the highest point of Caras Galadhon, you have the vast expanse of the
golden forest below and the great open realms of the skies above. The golden and
green colours of Lothlorien reach out towards the horizon where they meet with
the colours of the sky. Far far away in the distant east you can barely make out
the vague silver band of the great river Anduin, and to the south you see the
joyful race of the silvery Celebrant as it strives to catch up.
name: A Small Flet beneath the Sky
